lessons.title: Does this sound familiar?
lessons.subtitle: You ask a quick question and get an answer that totally misses what you needed.

cards.result: You get a dense architecture diagram. Not what you needed.
Claude replies: "What code?" You forgot to paste it.
You get a 20-page tutorial when you just wanted hooks explained.

lessons.footer: The culprit? Vague prompts. Let's fix them.

lessons.title: What makes a prompt vague?
lessons.subtitle: A few things every vague prompt is missing:

quadrants.label: No Role
quadrants.body: You don't tell the LLM who it's supposed to be. It defaults to a generic assistant with no expertise.

quadrants.label: No Context
quadrants.body: You don't share your level, situation, or what you're working on. The LLM guesses — and usually guesses wrong.

quadrants.label: No Clear Task
quadrants.body: Your ask is too broad or ambiguous. You get an overview, not the specific help you need.

quadrants.label: No Format
quadrants.body: You don't say how long or what shape the answer should be. You get a wall of text you didn't ask for.

lessons.title: The RCTF Framework
lessons.subtitle: Four parts. One prompt. Better every time.

pillars.name: Role
pillars.desc: Tell the LLM who it is.

lessons.example: Example: "You are a senior backend engineer [R]. I'm a CS student new to Kafka [C]. Explain consumer groups in 3 bullets [T]. No jargon [F]."

before.label: BEFORE  (vague)
bullets: Wall of text with architecture diagrams
No mention of your level or needs

after.label: AFTER  (RCTF structured)
after.prompt: "You are a senior backend engineer. I am a CS student new to Kafka. Explain consumer groups in 3 bullet points. Avoid jargon."
bullets: Exactly what you needed, first try

lessons.title: Build-A-Prompt Challenge
lessons.scenario: You want to ask an LLM to help you prepare for a data analyst job interview at a tech company.
lessons.instructions: For each RCTF component, pick the option that belongs in the prompt.

options.text: "You are an experienced data science hiring manager"
"I am a CS student with SQL & Python skills, applying for a junior data analyst role"
"Give me the 5 most common interview questions and a model answer for each"
"Format as Q&A pairs, keep each answer under 80 words"
